description: A larger empid, with brownish-olive upperparts. Crown grayish and somewhat peaked, darker than the back. Dull incomplete white eye-ring somewhat broader posteriorly but not tear-shaped. Lores dull white. Dark eye. Sides of face and nape darker and grayer than the back. White chin and throat contrasting with the face. Whitish underparts with flanks and lower belly having a slight yellowish wash. The bill appeared wide and somewhat long and was completely yellow-orange on the lower mandible and blackish on the upper mandible. Fairly long wings, grayish-black in color with two obvious contrasting white wing-bars. Tertials and secondaries edged white on outer web, primaries tipped with narrow white. Medium-long primary extension. Broad, medium length tail. Upper tail similar in color to wings but not quite as dark. Legs and feet blackish.Behaviors: Calling and flycatching in low willows.Call: Quite loud "pip" note, heard often.Plumage: adult
photographer: Photographer: Rachel Hopper, Rachel Hopper, Rachel Hopper, Rachel Hopper
